Route builder — order, dates, book

Pick your start date, tick places and set nights for each — check-in and check-out dates are computed by chaining from the start date. Edit the # number to reorder: type 2 to slot a place into position 2, everything after shifts down and keeps its nights. The trip always ends back at Kyiv. The trip map above redraws your route as you go.

#PlaceNightsCheck-inCheck-outKmHWeatherBook

Km are driving distances between consecutive stops; some legs are estimated where no route data was available. Hours assume a 70 km/h trip average incl. short breaks. Rows marked alternative are researched options near the route — hover for why, tick to swap them in. Weather is the stay's high/low from Open-Meteo; dates too far out for a forecast show last year's same dates instead. Booking/Airbnb links open pre-filled searches — availability and prices are theirs.

The places — profiles

Every stop in one gallery. Places on your route in the builder above float to the top, numbered in visit order.

Banská Štiavnica

stop · SK

Nestled in the caldera of an ancient collapsed volcano, Banská Štiavnica is a beautifully preserved medieval silver mining town and UNESCO World Heritage site. Surrounded by the scenic Štiavnica Mountains and pristine artificial lakes called 'tajchy', it offers a perfect blend of rich history, hiking trails, and unique freshwater beach spots. It is an ideal base for exploring Slovakia's artistic heritage and dramatic natural landscapes.

Local food to try: Bryndzové halušky, Štiavnická medovina, Lokše

  • Where to eat
  • Pivovar ERB — Local craft beers paired with traditional Slovak dishes and fresh seasonal salads. ~€20/pp
  • Pizzeria Black M — Excellent wood-fired pizzas and large fresh salads in a cozy, casual setting. ~€12/pp
  • 4 Sochy — Fine dining in a historic building featuring beautifully plated modern European dishes and seafood options. ~€30/pp
  • In & around
  • Tajch Klinger — 10 min drive, historic swimming reservoir (~Free)
  • Slovak Mining Museum (Baňa Bartolomej) — 5 min drive, underground mine tour (~€10)
  • Sitno Peak Hike — 20 min drive, highest peak of Štiavnica Mountains (~Free)

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · sight — click a pin for details

  • Interesting facts
  • The town is built directly inside a massive, ancient volcanic caldera.
  • Its network of 'tajchy' reservoirs was an engineering marvel of the 18th century, designed to pump water out of deep silver mines.
  • Maria Theresa founded the world's first forestry and mining academy here in 1762.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • The historic center is exceptionally steep with slippery cobblestones; leave high heels behind and wear sturdy hiking or walking shoes.
  • Driving and parking in the old town center is heavily restricted; park at the designated Banská Belá or Nový Zámok lots to avoid hefty fines.
  • Most local museums, including the popular underground mining exhibition, are closed on Mondays.

More photos on Google Images →

Duino-Aurisina

stop · IT

Duino-Aurisina is a stunning coastal stretch on the Gulf of Trieste, where the dramatic white cliffs of the Karst Plateau plunge into the Adriatic Sea. It offers a perfect blend of history and nature, featuring the romantic Duino Castle, the scenic Rilke Trail, and secluded pebble beaches. It serves as an ideal base for exploring the Italian-Slovenian borderlands, rich in Austro-Hungarian heritage and unique culinary traditions.

Local food to try: Sardoni in saor, Jota, Terrano wine, Carso cheese

  • Where to eat
  • Ristorante Alla Dama Bianca — Fresh seafood platter and homemade pasta served right on the waterfront, ~€40/pp
  • Pizzeria Ristorante Al Porto — Excellent wood-fired pizzas and fresh Mediterranean salads overlooking Sistiana Bay, ~€20/pp
  • Osmiza Pipan Klarič — Traditional Karst cold cuts, local cheeses, and terrano wine served in a rustic farm setting, ~€15/pp
  • In & around
  • Duino Castle — 2 min drive, clifftop castle with sea views (~€10)
  • Giant Cave (Grotta Gigante) — 15 min drive, one of the world's largest tourist caves (~€15) · Book
  • Miramare Castle — 20 min drive, Habsburg palace on the waterfront (~€12) · Book
  • Rilke Trail — 5 min walk, scenic clifftop hiking path (~Free)

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · sight — click a pin for details

  • Interesting facts
  • The famous Bohemian-Austrian poet Rainer Maria Rilke wrote his 'Duino Elegies' while staying at Duino Castle.
  • The local white limestone from Aurisina quarries was used by the Romans to build Aquileia and later used in the construction of the Suez Canal.
  • The Timavo River flows underground for 40 kilometers through deep caves before resurfacing near Duino.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Many local beaches, like Spiaggia del Principe, require steep hikes down rocky paths; wear sturdy footwear.
  • Look out for red arrows pointing to 'Osmize'—temporary farm taverns offering cheap local wine and cured meats.
  • Parking near Sistiana Bay and Duino Castle is highly limited and strictly monitored during summer; carry coins for meters.
  • A car is essential for exploring the Karst plateau, but be aware of narrow, stone-walled roads in Aurisina village.

More photos on Google Images →

Tihany

stop · HU

Dramatically perched on a volcanic peninsula jutting into Lake Balaton, Tihany captivates with its twin-towered Benedictine Abbey and fragrant lavender fields. This historic village serves as an ideal base for hiking volcanic trails, relaxing on grassy lakeside beaches, and exploring rich local art galleries. It is a peaceful yet culturally vibrant escape showcasing the very best of Hungary's inland sea.

Local food to try: Balatoni fogas, Halászlé, Tihanyi levendulás sör, Lángos

  • Where to eat
  • Ferenc Pince Csárda — Traditional Hungarian fish soup (Halászlé) and grilled pike-perch, ~8000 HUF/pp
  • Karolina Fűszerkertje — Upscale lakeside dining featuring fresh Mediterranean-style salads and local catfish, ~11000 HUF/pp
  • Apátsági Rege Cukrászda — Famous lavender-infused cream cake paired with panoramic lake views, ~3500 HUF/pp
  • In & around
  • Balatonfüred — 15 min drive, elegant lakeside promenade and Tagore Walkway
  • Szigliget Castle — 50 min drive, medieval fortress ruins with panoramic lake views (~2000 HUF)
  • Festetics Palace — 1 hr 10 min drive, grand baroque palace and museums in Keszthely (~3200 HUF)

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · sight — click a pin for details

  • Interesting facts
  • The crypt of the Benedictine Abbey houses the 1055 tomb of King Andrew I, the only medieval Hungarian king's burial site that remains completely undisturbed.
  • Tihany was designated as Hungary's very first landscape protection area in 1952 due to its unique volcanic geyser cones and rare flora.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Parking in the historic village center is strictly restricted; use the designated pay-and-display lots at the entrance of the peninsula to avoid heavy fines.
  • Summer evenings near the lake bring dense swarms of mosquitoes, so carry high-quality insect repellent for outdoor dining.
  • The narrow streets get extremely crowded during the June Lavender Festival; arrive before 9:00 AM to secure parking and beat the midday heat.
